programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels to the 'wild west' of Montana, USA, to visit a remarkable habitation perched on one of the region's highest mountains - home to the Bellrose family, the highest-altitude residents ever featured in the series. There he meets Brandon and Alisa and their four children, who have spent five years battling a brutal climate where daily life can be a matter of survival. Now, the family find themselves at a dramatic crossroads. Ben joins the off-grid clan in their cramped RV as they work toward launching a new business for winter sports enthusiasts. programme.title: Catching Up With The Royals with Rev Richard Coles
programme.desc: Hosted by the Reverend Richard Coles and royal correspondent Emily Andrews, this series delves beyond the pomp and pageantry of royal life, revealing what's really going on behind the headlines of the world's most famous family - the Windsors. Each week, Richard and Emily give their take on the latest royal tattle - from the moments everyone is talking about, to the stories quietly unfolding behind palace walls. Emily's years travelling with King Charles, Prince William, and Harry and Meghan, paired with Richard's passion for royal history and relationships, bring you insider whispers you won't hear anywhere else. Prod: Laura Pierce; Spirit Studios (S1 Ep1)

programme.title: Dad's Army: 55 Years of Laughs
programme.desc: This show celebrates all the iconic catchphrases, the hilarious stunts and goes behind the scenes to discover what happened when the cameras stopped rolling.

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Traditions
programme.desc: We look at the shocking events that led to the famous tradition of the State Opening - the ceremony of the Black Rod. We reveal the secrets of more everyday yet vital royal traditions that have become part of the world around us - from the centuries' old tradition of naming things after senior royals- everything from pubs to streets to American states- to the origins of the vast Royal Collection of art, started by a king who lost his head. We also explore the essential role of planning the line of royal succession decades before a monarch dies, and what happens when a queen breaks with tradition and refuses to open Parliament. Plus, a visit to Kew Palace reveals the traditional and horrific 'cures' inflicted on George III as he dealt with his secret 'madness'. Prod/Dir: Simon Newton; Series Prod/Dir: Michael Douglas; Exec Prod: Oliver Wright (S2 Ep2)
Revealing how the monarchy uses traditions to maintain a place in our nation's identity.

programme.title: The Assassinations of Queen Victoria
programme.desc: This programme tells the extraordinary true story of eight separate attempts on Queen Victoria's life during her 63-year reign. It's a gripping portrait of danger, defiance, and the unexpected threats lurking behind royal pageantry. Victoria , crowned at 18, married and expecting by 21, insisted on showing herself to the crowds. But visibility came with grave danger. On Constitution Hill, a gunman fired at her point blank. She stayed calm, told her driver to move on, and the would be assassin was hauled to Bedlam, while the Queen's bravery sent her popularity soaring. The assassination attempt set a pattern for seven further astonishing attacks on Victoria's life. With the Queen refusing to take precautions, a succession of disillusioned, politically motivated and deluded young men took aim at Victoria. And as each plot grew bolder, one question haunted the nation: how long could the Queen's luck hold? Series Prod: Graeme Hart; Prod/Dir: John McLaverty; Exec Prod: Pauline Law; IWC Media
